raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "assertive reframing of suffering as internally generated to promote cognitive accountability",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "suffering always has a single causal pathway",
    "thoughts can be objectively classified as 'wrong'",
    "no suffering arises from external events or non-cognitive factors",
    "eliminating wrong thought necessarily eliminates suffering"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally consistent as a universal causal claim but logically brittle due to the absolute quantifier 'always'",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "patient's distress is primarily maintained by identifiable cognitive distortions; therapeutic relationship supports exploration without invalidation; cultural context values personal agency over systemic explanation",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "real external harms, trauma, or physiological conditions are present; statement is used to enforce self-blame or deny legitimate grievance; patient lacks resources to alter material circumstances"
}
